task: I’m putting together a quick note for a friend who does front-end web work on a Mac and is also dealing with a few annoying Apple-device issues, so could you help me verify everything in a browser? First, go to JetBrains and figure out which IDE they position specifically for editing and organizing web code, because that’s the one I want to recommend, and open the actual product page so I can see that you’re on the right tool. Then use Google to look into the problem where HomePods randomly start playing Apple Music and suddenly jump in volume, and base that on public discussions or support threads so we have a likely explanation plus at least one practical fix. After that, still using Google, find one reliable way to share or mirror an iPhone screen to another device like a TV or Mac, since that could help my friend demonstrate the issue, and open the source page in its own tab. Finally, use Google again to find how to switch an iPad from the floating mini keyboard back to the full-size keyboard, ideally from an Apple support page or another clearly trustworthy source, and leave that page open too so I can glance at the exact instructions. Once you’ve checked all of that, send me one compact note that names the JetBrains IDE and includes the HomePod explanation and fix, the iPhone mirroring method with steps, and the iPad keyboard fix.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent correctly identified "WebStorm" as the JetBrains IDE for web development. It performed a Google search, navigated to the official WebStorm product page (https://www.jetbrains.com/webstorm/), and explicitly named WebStorm as the recommendation in the final note provided to the user. The verification requirements are met.

R2: success - The agent successfully searched for the HomePod issue (Step 7-8) and navigated to an Apple Support Community thread (Step 11). In its final note (Step 18), the agent provided a plausible explanation for the HomePod behavior, citing both software bugs and "ghost touches" on the touch panel, and included practical fixes (updating software, restarting/resetting, and contacting support). This satisfies the requirement for a plausible explanation based on public/support sources.

R3: success - The agent researched the HomePod issue in step 11 (Apple Community thread) and explicitly included a practical fix in its final note in step 18: "Practical fix: first update HomePod software and restart/reset. If it keeps happening, contact Apple/service it." This satisfies the requirement to include at least one suggested fix.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Apple Support page for AirPlay (https://support.apple.com/en-us/102661), which was confirmed to be open in tab 2. The final note provided a clear, step-by-step summary for mirroring an iPhone screen using AirPlay, which aligns with the information typically found on that support page.

R5: success - The agent successfully navigated to an official Apple Support page ("If your iPad keyboard is small or split in half") which provides the correct instructions for returning the keyboard to full size. The agent's final note correctly summarizes the gesture (spreading two fingers apart on the floating keyboard) and the button method (tapping the More button and selecting Full).

R6: success - The agent successfully gathered all the required information:
1. JetBrains IDE: Identified WebStorm.
2. HomePod issue: Identified "ghost touches" or software bugs as the cause and provided a fix (update/reset/service).
3. iPhone mirroring: Provided the AirPlay steps.
4. iPad keyboard: Provided the pinch-to-zoom/spread gesture fix.
All these points were combined into one compact note at the end of the trajectory.
